english: lug-tóṅ (lug tong) Sch. wether. — lúg-pa (lug pa) 1. sbst. shepherd, keeper of sheep Ma. 2. to stick the heads together like timid sheep, to be sheepish in behaviour Ma. — lug-míg (lug mig) n. of a flower Med. — lug-múr (lug mur) and lug-rtsí (lug rtsi) medicinal herbs. — lug-tságs (lug tsags) a sheep-skin with little wool on it Ld. — lug-rú (lug ru) ram's horn; n. of several species of Pedicularis. — *lug-lóg* (*lug log*) sheep-skin Ld.
I. sbst. and vb., v. sub lug (lug). — II. vb., to give way, to fall down, cf. rlúg-pa (rlug pa) Ts.

1. the casting, founding, of metal, lúgs-su blúg-pa (lugs su blug pa) Glr., *lúg-la lúg-pa* (*lug la lug pa*) col. C., to found, to cast; lúgs-ma (lugs ma) a cast, rgya-gár lúgs-ma (rgya gar lugs ma) an image (statue, idol) cast in India Glr. — 2. way, manner, fashion, mode, method, bód-kyi lúgs su gyis śig (bod kyi lugs su gyis shig) Glr. make it according to the fashion of Tibet; ṅai lúgs-kyis bon byed dgos (ngai lugs kyis bon byed dgos) you must live according to our, i.e. the Bon-fashion Mil.; bsam-gtán-la yód-pai lúgs-su byas (bsam gtan la yod pai lugs su byas) he feigned meditation Glr.; di yín-pai lúgs-su byed (di yin pai lugs su byed) they speak, act, make it appear, as if it really were so Tar. 184, 21; ṅa-ráṅ-gi lúgs-kyi mkár-las (nga rang gi lugs kyi mkhar las) my way of building, what I call my style of building Mil.; opinion, view, judgment, way of proceeding, kyed-ráṅ-gi lúgs-la (khyed rang gi lugs la) according to you, if we followed your advice Mil.; čós-lugs (chos lugs) religion, i.e. a certain system of faith and worship, p̱yi naṅ γnyis čós-lugs gaṅ bzaṅ (phyi nang gnyis chos lugs gang bzang) which of the two religions, the Brahman or the Buddhist be the better one Glr.; established manner, custom, usage, rite, čá-lugs (cha lugs) mode of dress, fashion, čós-lugs (chos lugs) religious rites, rgyá-lugs (rgya lugs) Chinese (or Indian) manners, bód-lugs (bod lugs) Tibetan manners etc.; ráṅ-lugs (rang lugs) one's own way, gźán-lugs (gzhan lugs) other people's way or manners; ráṅ-lugs-la gnás-pa (rang lugs la gnas pa) (= raṅ-sa ₒdzin-pa (rang sa 'dzin pa)) Glr.; seems to be only another expression for that Buddhist virtue of absolute indifference to all objects of the outer world; lugs (lugs) is also used concretely, meaning the adherents of a custom or religion, hence = sect, school, religious party, denomination, mdo-lugs (mdo lugs) follower of the Sutras, the Sutra sect, sṅags-lugs (sngags lugs) a follower of the Tantras, the Tantra sect; in a special sense: lugs γnyis (lugs gnyis) the two principal classes with regard to religious life, ₒjig-rtén-gyi lugs ('jig rten gyi lugs) the laical or profane class, laymen, čós-kyi lugs (chos kyi lugs) the clerical or sacred class, priests Cs.; lúgs-kyi (lugs kyi) that which relates to manners or morals, ethical Cs. (v. As. Res. XX, 583). — 3. in conjunction with a verbal root or with the genit. of the inf. it often corresponds to the English termination ing as: ldáṅ-lugs (ldang lugs) the rising, getting up, ₒgró-lugs ('gro lugs) the going, sdód-lugs (sdod lugs) the sitting Mil., ₒo-rgyál yóṅ-lugs sogs śól-ₒdebs-kyi źí-ba ('o rgyal yong lugs sogs shol 'debs kyi zhi ba) the (possibility of) getting into difficulties and other reasons for inducing him to postpone (his setting out) Mil.; bsam-yás bźéṅs-lugs bris (bsam yas bzhengs lugs bris) he described the building of Sam-yé (bsam yas) Glr.; méd-lugs (med lugs) the (circumstance of) not having Mil.; yín-lugs (yin lugs) the condition, state Mil.; dá-lta ná-lugs ći-ltar na (da lta na lugs ci ltar na) as to your present illness, in what does it consist? Mil.; tsógs-nas skyóṅ-bai lúgs-su yód-pa (tshogs nas skyong bai lugs su yod pa) they joined in educating them, they educated them together Mil.; it is also added to adjectives: čé-lugs (che lugs) greatness Mil.

I. 1. a strap, slung over the shoulder or round the waist, for carrying things; handle, ear (curved), of vessels, baskets etc., different from yú-ba (yu ba) a straight handle, hilt. — 2. 'foot-stalk of fruits' Cs.; luṅ-tag (lung thag) Cs.: a rosary, string of beads, suspended by the girdle. II. = bka (bka'), used of words spoken by secular persons commanding respect: pas γnáṅ-bai luṅ tób-nas (phas gnang bai lung thob nas) obtaining (his) father's word of permission Dzl., luṅ byuṅ (lung byung) an order is issued (by the king) Glr., tú-ru-śka-la luṅ len dgós-pa (tu ru shka la lung len dgos pa) being obliged to accept orders from the Turuskas Tar.; more frq.: spiritual exhortation, admonition, instruction, luṅ γnáṅ-ba (lung gnang ba) to give it (sometimes only: to pronounce forms of prayer etc. before devotees); luṅ gtóṅ-ba (lung gtong ba) id., *luṅ taṅ-kén* (*lung tang ken*) instructor, teacher, admonisher C.; luṅ stón-pa (lung ston pa), also lúṅ-du stón-pa (lung du ston pa) to instruct, to give spiritual precepts, also with regard <
